commands.meaning: Shows Windows network adapter IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, DNS servers, DHCP, and MAC details.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The Windows endpoint appears to have an APIPA address.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Windows assigned itself a 169.254.x.x address because it likely did not receive a DHCP lease.
possible_causes: Port security or NAC blocking DHCP
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assign a static IP until VLAN, DHCP scope, and gateway details are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: A Windows network adapter is disconnected.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The adapter is not detecting a network link or active connection.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change IP settings until the physical or wireless link is confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The endpoint may not have a default gateway.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Without a default gateway, the endpoint may reach only the local subnet.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change gateway settings until the correct subnet and VLAN are confirmed.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the endpoint can reach its default gateway.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway IP.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the endpoint has learned the gateway MAC address.
look_for_good: Gateway IP has a MAC address

next_commands.why: Checks the local routing table and default route.
next_commands.meaning: Displays IPv4 and IPv6 routes configured on the endpoint.

commands.learning_note: APIPA usually means the endpoint failed to get DHCP; gateway and ARP checks separate DHCP issues from Layer 2 reachability issues.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Tests basic reachability to an IP address or hostname using ICMP echo.
use_for: Compare DNS name and IP tests

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The ping test did not receive replies.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The target may be unreachable, blocking ICMP, offline, or separated by a network issue.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assume the service is down based only on ICMP; compare with gateway and DNS checks.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The local system or gateway reports the destination is unreachable.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may lack a valid route, gateway, or Layer 2 path.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change routes until IP, mask, gateway, and adapter state are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The hostname could not be resolved.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The ping did not reach the network test stage because DNS/name resolution failed.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change DNS settings until nslookup and IP connectivity are compared.

next_commands.why: Checks local IP, gateway, DNS, and adapter state.
Finds where the path stops after a ping failure.
next_commands.meaning: Shows route hops toward the target.

next_commands.why: Checks DNS resolution when pinging a name fails.
next_commands.meaning: Asks DNS for the hostname's address.

commands.learning_note: Ping failure can be network, DNS, host, route, or firewall; compare name, IP, gateway, and traceroute results.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Shows hop-by-hop path toward a target using TTL-limited probes.
bad_output: Unable to resolve target system name
warning_patterns: Unable to resolve target system name
critical_patterns: Unable to resolve target system name

patterns: Unable to resolve target system name
diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: Traceroute could not resolve the target name.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: DNS failed before route testing began.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change routes before DNS resolution is checked.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The path contains one or more timeout hops.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Some devices do not reply to traceroute probes; repeated early timeouts are more concerning.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assume a single timeout hop is a fault if later hops continue.

next_commands.why: Checks local gateway reachability before upstream path analysis.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway.
Shows local routes and default gateway.

commands.learning_note: Traceroute is strongest when compared with known-good path expectations and gateway reachability.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Shows the local ARP cache mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses.
use_for: Find stale or missing ARP clues
good_output: Gateway IP has a physical address

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The ARP cache has no visible entries.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may not have recently communicated with local subnet devices, including the gateway.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change adapter configuration until gateway reachability is tested.

next_commands.why: Generates traffic to the gateway and tests reachability.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway IP.

next_commands.why: Checks the configured gateway and adapter state.

commands.learning_note: ARP confirms local Layer 2 neighbor resolution, not internet reachability.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Shows the Windows routing table and interface route metrics.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The routing table appears to have no active routes.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may not be able to reach local or remote networks normally.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not add static routes until adapter and DHCP state are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The pasted output contains route table information.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Review the default route and interface metric to confirm outbound path selection.
possible_causes: No obvious route failure is visible from this output alone
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change persistent routes without approval and rollback details.

next_commands.why: Maps routes back to adapter IP, gateway, and DNS configuration.
Tests the default gateway shown in the route table.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway.

commands.learning_note: A route table explains where Windows will send traffic; it does not prove the next hop is reachable.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Queries DNS for hostname resolution and shows the DNS server used.

diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may not be able to reach its configured DNS server.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change DNS settings until IP connectivity to the DNS server is checked.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: DNS responded that the name was not found.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The queried hostname may be wrong or missing from DNS.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change endpoint DNS servers until the hostname and zone are confirmed.

next_commands.why: Checks which DNS servers the endpoint is using.
next_commands.meaning: Shows DNS server assignment and suffixes.

next_commands.why: Compares IP connectivity with DNS name resolution.
next_commands.meaning: Tests reachability to an IP address.

commands.learning_note: DNS troubleshooting is strongest when you compare name lookup, direct IP reachability, and configured DNS server reachability.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.

commands.meaning: Shows Windows network interface administrative and connection state.
good_output: Enabled and Connected for expected interface

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: A Windows network interface appears disabled.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The adapter will not pass traffic while disabled.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not enable adapters without confirming which network they connect to.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: A Windows network interface appears disconnected.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The adapter is enabled but not currently connected.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change IP settings until physical or wireless connectivity is checked.

next_commands.why: Checks detailed adapter IP and gateway assignment.
Checks whether the connected adapter can reach its gateway.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway IP.

commands.learning_note: Adapter state explains whether the endpoint can even attempt IP communication.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against vendor documentation for platform-specific differences.
